meta.description: BRS hub dietary and integrated lifestyle priority rollups — PM §4.1.1, §4.1.3 KCs, §4.2; curated Lifestyle Priorities in scripts/data/brs-hub-lifestyle-priorities.mjs.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Blueberries
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Polyphenol-rich fruit supporting FAAH-mediated endocannabinoid preservation and diet-sensitive modulation of stress-buffering capacity.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Eggs
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Phospholipid- and choline-rich whole food supporting NAPE → NAE biosynthesis and dietary entry into endocannabinoidome precursor pools.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Fish Roe
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Phospholipid-bound DHA source supporting NAPE biosynthesis, omega-3-derived endocannabinoidome signalling, and dopamine-linked neuromodulation.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Oats
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Whole-grain phospholipid and fibre matrix supporting NAPE precursor availability and endocannabinoid stress-buffering through regular meal-pattern intake.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Salmon
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Omega-3-rich oily fish supporting DHEA/EPEA-related endocannabinoidome signalling, dopamine neuromodulation, and stress-buffering lipid mediator context.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Walnuts
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Plant source of ALA and polyphenols supporting omega-3-derived endocannabinoidome signalling without reliance on pharmacological receptor targeting.

lifestyle.action: Prioritise sleep regularity and stress recovery
lifestyle.explanation: to support endocannabinoid tone, stress buffering, and healthier dopamine-related motivation context.
lifestyle.label: Prioritise sleep regularity and stress recovery to support endocannabinoid tone, stress buffering, and healthier dopamine-related motivation context.

lifestyle.action: Maintain regular meal patterns with phospholipid- and omega-3-rich foods
lifestyle.explanation: to support dietary entry into endocannabinoid precursor pools and related signalling context.
lifestyle.label: Maintain regular meal patterns with phospholipid- and omega-3-rich foods to support dietary entry into endocannabinoid precursor pools and related signalling context.

lifestyle.action: Limit ultra-processed, low-omega-3 dietary patterns
lifestyle.explanation: to help preserve endocannabinoid-related signalling context supported by whole-food fat quality.
lifestyle.label: Limit ultra-processed, low-omega-3 dietary patterns to help preserve endocannabinoid-related signalling context supported by whole-food fat quality.

key_constraints.commentary: Key dietary constraints include phospholipid-rich whole foods, choline and NAPE precursor availability, and omega-3-supportive patterns that sustain endogenous lipid mediator formation.

BRS-X(ECS).dietary_strategy_prose: Prioritise phospholipid-rich whole foods — eggs, oily fish, soy, and nuts — alongside choline-containing foods that support endocannabinoid precursor biology. Include omega-3-rich seafoods and a diverse range of polyphenol-rich plants to support endogenous lipid mediator formation through food rather than pharmacological receptor targeting.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Barley
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Soluble-fibre grain supporting microbial hormone biotransformation, testosterone signalling stability, and androgen–microbiome regulation.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Broccoli
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Cruciferous vegetable providing sulphur compounds and fibre that support androgen–microbiome regulation and microbial hormone metabolism.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Eggs
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Complete protein and micronutrient source supporting oestrogen signalling stability, metabolic–reproductive integration, and hormonal milieu through food-first patterns.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Oats
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Fermentable-fibre whole grain supporting estrobolome regulation, progesterone-supportive microbial metabolism, and metabolic–reproductive hormone integration.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Spinach
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Folate- and magnesium-rich leafy green supporting one-carbon and micronutrient context for hormone synthesis, clearance, and signalling stability.

lifestyle.action: Maintain regular physical activity, including post-meal movement
lifestyle.explanation: to support insulin sensitivity, metabolic stability, and healthier hormone signalling context.
lifestyle.label: Maintain regular physical activity, including post-meal movement to support insulin sensitivity, metabolic stability, and healthier hormone signalling context.

lifestyle.action: Prioritise sleep and stress recovery
lifestyle.explanation: to help stabilise neuroendocrine context relevant to reproductive hormone balance and luteal-phase recovery.
lifestyle.label: Prioritise sleep and stress recovery to help stabilise neuroendocrine context relevant to reproductive hormone balance and luteal-phase recovery.

lifestyle.action: Emphasise repeated fermentable-fibre patterns over short probiotic bursts
lifestyle.explanation: to support microbial steroid metabolism, estrobolome stability, and durable gut–hormone integration.
lifestyle.label: Emphasise repeated fermentable-fibre patterns over short probiotic bursts to support microbial steroid metabolism, estrobolome stability, and durable gut–hormone integration.

lifestyle.action: Limit antibiotic overuse and ultra-processed low-fibre eating
lifestyle.explanation: to help preserve microbial ecology that supports healthy steroid and androgen-related metabolism.
lifestyle.label: Limit antibiotic overuse and ultra-processed low-fibre eating to help preserve microbial ecology that supports healthy steroid and androgen-related metabolism.

key_constraints.commentary: Key dietary constraints include fermentable fibre and prebiotic substrate, diverse plant-polyphenol intake, and protein and micronutrient sufficiency supporting microbial hormone metabolism.

BRS-X(Hormones).dietary_strategy_prose: Include fermentable fibre from vegetables, legumes, and whole grains to support microbial hormone metabolism. Eat a diverse range of plant foods across the week, phytoestrogen-containing whole foods where relevant to life stage, and adequate protein and micronutrients to support hormonal milieu through food-first patterns.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Blueberries
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Polyphenol-rich fruit that supports glutamate clearance, recycling, and excitotoxicity modulation within GABA–glutamate regulation.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Eggs
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Provides complete protein, choline, and membrane lipids that support amino-acid precursor supply, acetylcholine synthesis, and neuronal DHA incorporation across BRS1 pathways.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Extra Virgin Olive Oil
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Provides monounsaturated fats and polyphenols that support membrane context and glutamate clearance pathways within neuronal signalling balance.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Fish Roe
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Delivers phospholipid-bound DHA in a food matrix suited to neuronal membrane incorporation rather than isolated triglyceride omega-3 alone.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Greek Yogurt (plain, unsweetened)
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Fermented dairy providing protein matrix and microbial metabolites that support GABA synthesis capacity within the gut–neurotransmitter interface.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Lentils
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Plant protein source that supports amino-acid pool sufficiency and complementary amino-acid balance when paired with grains, relevant to neurotransmitter precursor availability.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Pumpkin Seeds
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Magnesium- and zinc-rich seed supporting GABA–glutamate balance and GABA synthesis capacity within excitatory–inhibitory regulation.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Salmon
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Rich in DHA and phospholipid-bound omega-3s that support neuronal membrane incorporation and help modulate excitatory–inhibitory balance under inflammatory or oxidative load.

lifestyle.action: Maintain regular meal timing and circadian alignment
lifestyle.explanation: to support a steady supply of neurotransmitter building blocks, metabolic regulation, and balanced brain signalling throughout the day.
lifestyle.label: Maintain regular meal timing and circadian alignment to support a steady supply of neurotransmitter building blocks, metabolic regulation, and balanced brain signalling throughout the day.

lifestyle.action: Include sufficient protein at main meals
lifestyle.explanation: to help maintain reliable amino-acid availability and support neurotransmitter precursor supply across the day.
lifestyle.label: Include sufficient protein at main meals to help maintain reliable amino-acid availability and support neurotransmitter precursor supply across the day.

lifestyle.action: Prioritise sufficient, consistent sleep
lifestyle.explanation: to support balanced neurotransmitter regulation, cognitive performance, and physiological recovery.
lifestyle.label: Prioritise sufficient, consistent sleep to support balanced neurotransmitter regulation, cognitive performance, and physiological recovery.

lifestyle.action: Practise regular stress-management techniques
lifestyle.explanation: to help maintain healthy stress recovery, balanced autonomic activity, and adaptive neurochemical signalling.
lifestyle.label: Practise regular stress-management techniques to help maintain healthy stress recovery, balanced autonomic activity, and adaptive neurochemical signalling.

lifestyle.action: Engage in regular physical activity
lifestyle.explanation: to support healthy neurotransmitter function, metabolic regulation, and long-term brain resilience.
lifestyle.label: Engage in regular physical activity to support healthy neurotransmitter function, metabolic regulation, and long-term brain resilience.

lifestyle.action: Prepare omega-3-rich foods gently and include them regularly
lifestyle.explanation: to protect delicate marine fats during cooking and support ongoing brain membrane health over time.
lifestyle.label: Prepare omega-3-rich foods gently and include them regularly to protect delicate marine fats during cooking and support ongoing brain membrane health over time.

key_constraints.commentary: Key dietary constraints include complete amino-acid availability through protein-inclusive meals, complementary food pairings where plant proteins are used, and steady supply of membrane and cofactor nutrients that support neurotransmitter synthesis and signalling.

BRS1.dietary_strategy_prose: Prioritise protein at each meal so the brain has a steady supply of amino-acid precursors for neurotransmitter synthesis. Include B-vitamin-rich foods, omega-3-containing seafoods, and healthy fats from olive oil, nuts, and eggs that support cell membranes and receptor function. Build meals that combine protein, fibre-rich carbohydrates, and healthy fats to keep energy steady and support attention-relevant signalling.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Eggs
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Concentrated source of choline, B12, and complete protein supporting homocysteine remethylation, SAMe synthesis, transsulfuration, and phosphatidylcholine formation.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Fish Roe
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Phospholipid-rich seafood supporting phosphatidylcholine formation and the methylation–membrane coupling axis.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Mackerel
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Oily fish providing B vitamins and phospholipid substrate that support glutathione synthesis and choline–phosphatidylcholine pathways.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Pumpkin Seeds
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Magnesium- and zinc-rich seed supporting SAMe synthesis and enzymatic cofactor sufficiency across methylation-cycle reactions.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Salmon
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Provides B vitamins, selenium, and phospholipid context supporting glutathione synthesis and choline-derived phosphatidylcholine formation.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Sardines
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Oily fish supplying B12, selenium, and phospholipid-bound fats that support transsulfuration, glutathione synthesis, and membrane methylation coupling.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Spinach
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Leafy green rich in folate and betaine that supports folate–B12 remethylation, BHMT-mediated remethylation, and methyl-donor throughput across the one-carbon cycle.

lifestyle.action: Maintain regular daily meal timing
lifestyle.explanation: to support steady methyl-donor and one-carbon nutrient availability across the day.
lifestyle.label: Maintain regular daily meal timing to support steady methyl-donor and one-carbon nutrient availability across the day.

lifestyle.action: Prioritise sleep and stress recovery
lifestyle.explanation: to help manage methylation demand and support broader one-carbon metabolism, alongside dietary substrate supply.
lifestyle.label: Prioritise sleep and stress recovery to help manage methylation demand and support broader one-carbon metabolism, alongside dietary substrate supply.

key_constraints.commentary: Key dietary constraints include adequate methyl-donor intake from folate-, B12-, choline-, and betaine-rich whole foods, protein spread across the day, and dietary patterns that prevent chronic shortfall in methyl-donor availability.

BRS2.dietary_strategy_prose: Include folate-rich leafy greens, eggs, legumes, and liver where appropriate; choline from eggs, fish, and soy; and betaine from beets and spinach. Spread protein across the day rather than concentrating it in one meal. Choose whole foods that retain their B vitamins and methyl donors instead of highly processed alternatives that strip them away.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Barley
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Soluble-fibre whole grain supporting fermentable substrate delivery, gut-derived inflammatory signalling modulation, and SCFA-related anti-inflammatory context.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Blueberries
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Anthocyanin-rich fruit supporting ROS generation–clearance balance, NF-κB modulation, and endogenous antioxidant defences.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Broccoli
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Sulphur-rich crucifer providing glucosinolate-derived compounds and antioxidant cofactors that support redox stability and inflammatory resolution capacity.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Extra Virgin Olive Oil
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Monounsaturated fat and polyphenol source that supports anti-inflammatory signalling tone and reduces reliance on oxidised dietary fats.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Fermented Vegetables
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Live-culture foods supporting gut-derived inflammatory signalling modulation and microbial metabolite context for systemic inflammatory tone.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Salmon
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: EPA- and DHA-rich oily fish supporting NF-κB regulation, cytokine network modulation, and resolution-oriented lipid mediator formation.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Spinach
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Leafy green supplying vitamins C and E, folate, and magnesium that support antioxidant network recycling and cytokine network balance.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Walnuts
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Plant source of ALA and polyphenols that supports antioxidant network recycling and a favourable omega-6:omega-3 balance within inflammatory tone.

lifestyle.action: Maintain consistent daily eating patterns
lifestyle.explanation: to support lower inflammatory load and more stable antioxidant and resolution capacity over time.
lifestyle.label: Maintain consistent daily eating patterns to support lower inflammatory load and more stable antioxidant and resolution capacity over time.

lifestyle.action: Prioritise sleep and manage stress load
lifestyle.explanation: to help reduce systemic inflammatory pressure and support healthier immune and gut-related signalling.
lifestyle.label: Prioritise sleep and manage stress load to help reduce systemic inflammatory pressure and support healthier immune and gut-related signalling.

lifestyle.action: Reduce exposure to smoke, pollution, and excess alcohol
lifestyle.explanation: to lower avoidable oxidative and inflammatory burden on the body's defence and repair systems.
lifestyle.label: Reduce exposure to smoke, pollution, and excess alcohol to lower avoidable oxidative and inflammatory burden on the body's defence and repair systems.

lifestyle.action: Choose gentler cooking and limit heavily fried or oxidised fats
lifestyle.explanation: to reduce dietary sources of oxidative stress and help protect membrane lipids and antioxidant capacity.
lifestyle.label: Choose gentler cooking and limit heavily fried or oxidised fats to reduce dietary sources of oxidative stress and help protect membrane lipids and antioxidant capacity.

lifestyle.action: Limit ultra-processed food exposure
lifestyle.explanation: to help reduce post-meal inflammatory burden and support a more anti-inflammatory dietary baseline.
lifestyle.label: Limit ultra-processed food exposure to help reduce post-meal inflammatory burden and support a more anti-inflammatory dietary baseline.

lifestyle.action: Include cruciferous vegetables and marine fats regularly
lifestyle.explanation: to support repeated dietary activation of antioxidant and resolution pathways rather than one-off intake.
lifestyle.label: Include cruciferous vegetables and marine fats regularly to support repeated dietary activation of antioxidant and resolution pathways rather than one-off intake.

key_constraints.commentary: Key dietary constraints include antioxidant substrate availability from diverse plant foods, balanced omega-3 and omega-6 fatty-acid intake, and dietary patterns that limit chronic inflammatory and oxidative load.

BRS3.dietary_strategy_prose: Centre meals on colourful vegetables, berries, herbs, and spices that provide natural antioxidants. Include oily fish, walnuts, flaxseed, and olive oil for omega-3 balance, and reduce reliance on ultra-processed foods and heavily fried or oxidised fats that add unnecessary inflammatory load. Eat a wide variety of plant foods rather than relying on high-dose isolated antioxidant supplements.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Blueberries
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Polyphenol-rich fruit supporting ROS control, mitochondrial resilience, and oxidative stress modulation within bioenergetic regulation.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Broccoli
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Cruciferous vegetable providing sulphur compounds and antioxidant cofactors that support mitochondrial resilience and redox stability under metabolic demand.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Eggs
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Rich in B vitamins, choline, and complete protein supporting cellular bioenergetics, cofactor sufficiency, and sustained substrate delivery to mitochondria.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Oats
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Slowly digested whole grain providing steady carbohydrate fuel and B-vitamin context for substrate utilisation flexibility and cognitive energy availability.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Pumpkin Seeds
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Magnesium- and zinc-rich seed supporting mitochondrial enzyme cofactors, redox stability, and cellular energy production pathways.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Salmon
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Oily fish providing CoQ10-relevant context, omega-3s, and high-quality protein supporting electron transport, ROS control, and mitochondrial biogenesis.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Spinach
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Iron-, magnesium-, and folate-rich leafy green supporting electron transport cofactors, redox balance, and mitochondrial enzyme function.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Walnuts
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: ALA and polyphenol source supporting mitochondrial biogenesis, ROS production–control balance, and metabolic fuel-switching context.

lifestyle.action: Engage in regular aerobic and resistance training
lifestyle.explanation: to raise energy demand, support mitochondrial adaptation, and improve metabolic fuel flexibility over time.
lifestyle.label: Engage in regular aerobic and resistance training to raise energy demand, support mitochondrial adaptation, and improve metabolic fuel flexibility over time.

lifestyle.action: Prioritise adequate sleep and recovery between activity
lifestyle.explanation: to support cellular energy metabolism, mitochondrial repair, and adaptive remodelling rather than chronic under-recovery.
lifestyle.label: Prioritise adequate sleep and recovery between activity to support cellular energy metabolism, mitochondrial repair, and adaptive remodelling rather than chronic under-recovery.

lifestyle.action: Maintain stable daily meal structure
lifestyle.explanation: to help keep energy substrates available and support steady mitochondrial throughput across the day.
lifestyle.label: Maintain stable daily meal structure to help keep energy substrates available and support steady mitochondrial throughput across the day.

lifestyle.action: Reduce oxidant exposure from smoke, pollution, and excess alcohol
lifestyle.explanation: to lower mitochondrial oxidative pressure and help preserve redox balance and protective capacity.
lifestyle.label: Reduce oxidant exposure from smoke, pollution, and excess alcohol to lower mitochondrial oxidative pressure and help preserve redox balance and protective capacity.

lifestyle.action: Choose gentler cooking methods
lifestyle.explanation: to reduce avoidable oxidative stress placed on mitochondrial protection and membrane integrity.
lifestyle.label: Choose gentler cooking methods to reduce avoidable oxidative stress placed on mitochondrial protection and membrane integrity.

lifestyle.action: Maintain consistent sleep–wake rhythms
lifestyle.explanation: to support biological pathways involved in cellular energy production and NAD-related metabolism.
lifestyle.label: Maintain consistent sleep–wake rhythms to support biological pathways involved in cellular energy production and NAD-related metabolism.

key_constraints.commentary: Key dietary constraints include steady energy from balanced meals across the day, sufficient B-vitamin, iron, and magnesium for mitochondrial function, and dietary patterns that avoid chronic energetic instability.

BRS4.dietary_strategy_prose: Build meals around dependable energy from protein, whole grains, legumes, and starchy vegetables. Include B-vitamin-rich foods, iron from lean meats and legumes, and magnesium from nuts, seeds, and greens to support how cells produce and use energy. Avoid long gaps without nourishment and favour minimally processed foods over options that leave energy pathways undersupported.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Barley
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Soluble-fibre grain promoting microbial fermentation, competitive ecological selection, and butyrate-supportive substrate for barrier and vagal context.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Blueberries
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Polyphenol-rich fruit supporting keystone taxa, microbial turnover, and biotransformation of plant compounds into gut- and mitochondria-relevant metabolites.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Fermented Vegetables
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Live-culture food providing postbiotic peptides and microbial signals that support vagal and enteric nervous system modulation.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Greek Yogurt (plain, unsweetened)
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Fermented dairy providing live cultures and protein matrix that support vagal–ENS signalling and structured meal patterns for gut rhythm stability.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Kefir
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Fermented dairy with diverse live cultures supporting vagal–ENS signalling and gut–brain communication through microbial and postbiotic pathways.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Oats
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: β-glucan-rich whole grain supporting fermentable fibre delivery, microbial ecological turnover, and SCFA production relevant to gut–brain signalling.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Salmon
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Omega-3-rich seafood supporting gut barrier tight-junction integrity and limiting gut-derived immune and inflammatory load.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Walnuts
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Polyphenol- and fibre-containing nut supporting microbial polyphenol biotransformation and metabolite generation relevant to gut–brain pathways.

lifestyle.action: Maintain consistent daily meal patterns
lifestyle.explanation: to support microbial rhythm stability, fermentation continuity, and steadier gut–brain signalling.
lifestyle.label: Maintain consistent daily meal patterns to support microbial rhythm stability, fermentation continuity, and steadier gut–brain signalling.

lifestyle.action: Prioritise sleep and manage stress
lifestyle.explanation: to help reduce indirect strain on gut barrier integrity, endotoxin load, and enteric signalling stability.
lifestyle.label: Prioritise sleep and manage stress to help reduce indirect strain on gut barrier integrity, endotoxin load, and enteric signalling stability.

lifestyle.action: Limit alcohol and ultra-processed food exposure
lifestyle.explanation: to reduce barrier strain and support a healthier gut environment for microbial and immune balance.
lifestyle.label: Limit alcohol and ultra-processed food exposure to reduce barrier strain and support a healthier gut environment for microbial and immune balance.

lifestyle.action: Emphasise repeated dietary pattern quality over short-term fixes
lifestyle.explanation: to support keystone microbes, SCFA production, and durable gut ecological turnover rather than isolated bursts.
lifestyle.label: Emphasise repeated dietary pattern quality over short-term fixes to support keystone microbes, SCFA production, and durable gut ecological turnover rather than isolated bursts.

lifestyle.action: Maintain dietary variety rather than overly restrictive eating
lifestyle.explanation: to support microbial diversity, competitive ecological selection, and long-term gut resilience.
lifestyle.label: Maintain dietary variety rather than overly restrictive eating to support microbial diversity, competitive ecological selection, and long-term gut resilience.

key_constraints.commentary: Key dietary constraints include adequate fermentable fibre, resistant starch, and diverse plant-food intake to support microbial diversity, microbial metabolism, and intestinal function.

BRS5.dietary_strategy_prose: Eat a wide variety of plant foods daily — vegetables, fruits, legumes, whole grains, nuts, and seeds — to feed beneficial gut microbes. Include fermented foods where tolerated, and fermentable fibre from oats, barley, beans, and cooled potatoes. Prioritise minimally processed meals that support the gut lining rather than ultra-processed foods that disrupt microbial balance and barrier function.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Barley
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: One of the richest whole-food sources of soluble fibre, promoting stable post-meal glucose responses, microbial fermentation, and metabolic flexibility.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Blueberries
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Polyphenol-rich fruit that supports vascular function, metabolic signalling, and oxidative resilience while providing a low-glycaemic whole-food carbohydrate source.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Chickpeas
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Low-glycaemic legume providing protein, fibre and resistant starch that help maintain metabolic stability and reduce post-prandial glucose excursions.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Extra Virgin Olive Oil
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Provides monounsaturated fats and polyphenols that support insulin sensitivity, reduce inflammatory burden, and complement healthy metabolic regulation.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Greek Yogurt (plain, unsweetened)
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Provides high-quality protein and a favourable food matrix that supports appetite regulation, metabolic control and structured meal composition.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Lentils
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Combines slowly digested carbohydrate, protein and fermentable fibre, supporting stable energy delivery, insulin regulation, and appetite control.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Oats
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Rich in β-glucan and slowly digested carbohydrates that support sustained energy availability, glycaemic stability, satiety, and favourable metabolic signalling.

nutrient_dense_stars.label: Salmon
nutrient_dense_stars.explanation: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids and high-quality protein, supporting metabolic resilience, neuroendocrine function, and recovery following physiological stress.

lifestyle.action: Take regular physical activity and, where practical, a short walk after meals
lifestyle.explanation: to help reduce blood sugar spikes after eating and support steadier metabolic regulation throughout the day.
lifestyle.label: Take regular physical activity and, where practical, a short walk after meals to help reduce blood sugar spikes after eating and support steadier metabolic regulation throughout the day.

lifestyle.action: Maintain consistent sleep and wake times
lifestyle.explanation: to support healthy cortisol rhythm, lower stress-driven eating pressure, and more stable metabolic recovery.
lifestyle.label: Maintain consistent sleep and wake times to support healthy cortisol rhythm, lower stress-driven eating pressure, and more stable metabolic recovery.

lifestyle.action: Practise stress-management and recovery techniques
lifestyle.explanation: to help maintain balanced autonomic activity, healthier appetite regulation, and adaptive stress responses.
lifestyle.label: Practise stress-management and recovery techniques to help maintain balanced autonomic activity, healthier appetite regulation, and adaptive stress responses.

lifestyle.action: Get morning daylight and limit evening artificial light
lifestyle.explanation: to strengthen circadian timing, support healthy sleep transitions, and align feeding with the daily light–dark cycle.
lifestyle.label: Get morning daylight and limit evening artificial light to strengthen circadian timing, support healthy sleep transitions, and align feeding with the daily light–dark cycle.

lifestyle.action: Maintain regular meal timing aligned with your daily rhythm
lifestyle.explanation: to support glucose tolerance, insulin sensitivity, and more predictable energy regulation across the day.
lifestyle.label: Maintain regular meal timing aligned with your daily rhythm to support glucose tolerance, insulin sensitivity, and more predictable energy regulation across the day.

lifestyle.action: Use slow breathing and recovery routines after stress or exertion
lifestyle.explanation: to support healthy transitions between activation and recovery and improve long-term autonomic flexibility.
lifestyle.label: Use slow breathing and recovery routines after stress or exertion to support healthy transitions between activation and recovery and improve long-term autonomic flexibility.

lifestyle.action: Support healthy body composition through sustainable nutrition, movement, sleep, and recovery
lifestyle.explanation: to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce adipose-related inflammatory signalling over time.
lifestyle.label: Support healthy body composition through sustainable nutrition, movement, sleep, and recovery to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce adipose-related inflammatory signalling over time.

key_constraints.commentary: Key dietary constraints include stable energy availability, dietary patterns that minimise blood sugar swings, consistent meal timing, and sufficient micronutrient and healthy fat intake to support metabolic adaptation and recovery.

BRS6.dietary_strategy_prose: Prioritise stable meal composition, consistent meal timing, and minimally processed foods that support sustained energy availability and metabolic flexibility. Build meals that combine protein, fibre-rich carbohydrates, and healthy fats; include protein-rich breakfasts where appropriate, polyphenol-rich plant foods, and omega-3-containing seafoods. Favour dietary patterns that minimise blood sugar swings and unnecessary inflammatory burden.
